_doc: State machine for the tax-validation loop. See tax-validation-loop.md for the procedure. Each cron fire processes ONE pending jurisdiction. mode: 'sweeping' (2h cadence, work remaining) or 'resting' (sweep complete). status per jurisdiction: 'pending' | 'ok' | 'flagged'. NEVER fabricate figures; competitor CONSENSUS or official DOR only; disagreement => flagged, no data change.
_compactNote: Each iteration increments `iteration`. When iteration % compactEvery == 0, the orchestrator must tell the user to run /compact. All loop state lives in THIS committed file, so /compact/clear/restart never lose progress. Processed so far: NY,NJ,VA,OH,MD,MN,WI,CT,OR,CA(2025-fallback),MO(2026),KS(2026),NE(2025-fallback),OK(2025-fallback),AR(2026),AL(2026),SC(2026),ME(2026),HI(2026),RI(2026). Next pending in queue: Delaware.

Minnesota.reason: Added as bracket type from the OFFICIAL Minnesota Dept. of Revenue. MN brackets are inflation-indexed (Minn. Stat. 270C.22), and CRITICALLY — unlike CA — the 2026 schedule IS officially published: DOR set 2026 amounts on Dec 16, 2025 (brackets +2.369% vs TY2025). Four statutory rates 5.35/6.80/7.85/9.85%. Single: 5.35% to $33,310, 6.80% to $109,430, 7.85% to $203,150, 9.85% above (std ded $15,300). MFJ: thresholds $48,700/$193,480/$337,930 (std ded $30,600). HoH: $41,010/$164,800/$270,060 (std ded $23,000). RESOLVED a year-mislabel trap: some secondary sources still show the 2025 single thresholds ($31,690/$183,340) under a 2026 label; official DOR (ranked first) overrides them. State-only; MN has no local income tax. MFS uses narrower brackets (disclaimed since our 'single' key bundles single+MFS). Dependent exemption/SS subtraction/credits not modeled. Scenario-tested via engine across $40k/$75k/$150k/$600k x single/married/HoH: all finite, non-negative, monotonic. Hand-verified single $75k -> $3,576.6050 (= 5.35% x 33310 + 6.80% x (59700-33310), taxable = 75000-15300), exact. npm test green (36+6 passing).
Minnesota.source: Official Minnesota Dept. of Revenue 2026 brackets press release (Dec 16, 2025) + Tax Year 2026 Inflation-Adjusted Amounts PDF (Tax Research Division, Dec 1, 2025); cross-checked vs competitor/news figures (which lagged at 2025 numbers). https://www.revenue.state.mn.us/press-release/2025-12-16/minnesota-income-tax-brackets-standard-deduction-and-dependent-exemption ; https://www.revenue.state.mn.us/sites/default/files/2025-12/inflation-adjusted-amounts-2026.pdf

Maryland.reason: Added as bracket type from the OFFICIAL Comptroller of Maryland Tax Alert (Revised Dec 22, 2025) implementing the Budget Reconciliation and Financing Act of 2025 (Ch. 604), effective for tax years beginning after Dec 31, 2024 (TY2025 AND 2026). The Act retains MD's statutory 2%-5.75% schedule (5.75% bracket capped at $250,001-$500,000 single / $300,001-$600,000 joint) and adds two NEW high-income brackets: 6.25% ($500,001-$1,000,000 single / $600,001-$1,200,000 joint) and 6.5% (over $1,000,000 single / over $1,200,000 joint). Standard deduction now a flat $3,350 single/MFS, $6,700 MFJ/HoH (phase-in eliminated, COLA-indexed going forward). Head of Household uses the joint-column thresholds + $6,700 std deduction per the Tax Alert. Modeled STATE ONLY. MD has COUNTY/Baltimore City LOCAL income tax (~2.25%-3.30%, varies by county) which is NOT modeled and disclaimed (it can rival/exceed the state tax). The 2% capital-gains surtax (FAGI > $350k) and personal exemptions/itemized phase-outs are disclaimed/not modeled. Scenario-tested via engine across $40k/$75k/$150k/$600k/$1.5M x single/married/HoH: all finite, non-negative, monotonic. Hand-verified single $75k -> $3,350.88 (= 20 + 30 + 40 + 4.75% of (71650-3000)), exact. npm test green (36 passing).
Maryland.source: Official Comptroller of Maryland Tax Alert (Rev. 12/22/2025) + Budget Reconciliation and Financing Act of 2025 (Ch. 604); validated vs Tax Foundation 2026 bracket data (full schedule + standard deduction match). https://www.marylandcomptroller.gov/content/dam/mdcomp/tax/legal-publications/alerts/tax-alert-changes-to-standard-and-itemized-deductions-and-to-state-and-local-income-tax-rates-from-the-2025-legislative-session.pdf ; https://taxfoundation.org/data/all/state/state-income-tax-rates-2026/

New York.reason: 2026 rates statutory (FY2026 NY budget: lowest 5 brackets cut 0.1% to 3.9/4.4/5.15/5.4/5.9%; brackets 6-9 unchanged, extended through 2032). Thresholds + std deduction carried from 2025 IT-201 (not indexed). Added as bracket type. NYC/Yonkers local + tax-benefit recapture not modeled (noted in disclaimer). Scenario-tested: $75k single -> $3,453 state tax, monotonic, finite.
New York.caveat: Re-verify lower-5 rates + HoH 6.85% threshold against official 2026 IT-201-I when DTF posts it (late 2026).

California.reason: ADDED under the APPROVED prior-year fallback policy (2026-06-16). The FTB has NOT published 2026 CA rate schedules and the 2026 Form 540-ES instructions direct 2026 filers to use the 2025 tax tables, so California is added as a bracket state with figureYear=2025 and an UNMISTAKABLE user-visible label. Re-confirmed the candidate2025 figures against official-FTB-sourced data: nine statutory rates 1/2/4/6/8/9.3/10.3/11.3/12.3%; SINGLE thresholds 11079/26264/41452/57542/72724/371479/445771/742953 (confirmed verbatim by LegalClarity; TaxFormFinder lists identical values but swaps its Schedule X/Y labels — values match candidate2025); MFJ ~2x single; HoH per Schedule Z (22173/52530/67716/83805/98990/505208/606251/1010417). 2025 standard deduction $5,540 single/MFS, $11,080 MFJ/HoH — the $5,706/$11,412 some sources show are the 2026 amounts and the 540-2EZ table credits (a different construct), NOT the 2025 Form 540 standard deduction; resolved that conflict via WebSearch consensus. The FTB PDFs are 403/WAF-blocked to WebFetch so secondary sources were used to re-confirm. USER-VISIBLE LABEL: build.js stateBody now emits a prominent '.year-fallback' banner whenever figureYear != taxYear; the rendered California page (dist/california-paycheck-calculator/index.html) shows 'YYYY rates (2026 pending). Showing California's official 2025 tax figures — the state has not published 2026 brackets yet and this page will update when 2026 figures are released.' Plus the disclaimer[0] repeats the 2025 notice in the 'What this estimate doesn't include' block. SIMPLIFICATIONS (disclaimed, state-only): the +1% Behavioral Health Services Tax over $1M (top effective 13.3%) is NOT modeled; personal/dependent exemption credits and itemized deductions not modeled. Scenario-tested via the engine across $40k/$75k/$150k/$600k x single/married/HoH: all finite, non-negative, monotonic. Hand-verified single $75k -> $2,940.85 exact (taxable = 75000-5540 = 69460; = 1%x11079 + 2%x(26264-11079) + 4%x(41452-26264) + 6%x(57542-41452) + 8%x(69460-57542)). npm test green (exit 0, all suites incl. tax-data freshness).
California.source: California 2025 Form 540 tax rate schedules (Schedule X/Y/Z) — official FTB figures re-confirmed vs LegalClarity + TaxFormFinder (brackets) and WebSearch consensus (2025 std deduction $5,540/$11,080). FTB PDFs 403-blocked to automated fetch. https://www.ftb.ca.gov/forms/2025/2025-540-tax-rate-schedules.pdf ; https://www.ftb.ca.gov/forms/2026/2026-540-es-instructions.html

candidate2025.note: FTB 2025 schedules; +1% Behavioral Health Services Tax over $1,000,000 (top 13.3%) not yet modeled by engine. Standard deduction 2025: 5540 single/MFS, 11080 MFJ/HoH. (2026 std deduction already published: 5706 / 11412.)

Virginia.reason: VA individual income tax is statutory (Va. Code § 58.1-320), 2%-5.75% at $3k/$5k/$17k thresholds, unchanged since 1990 and NOT inflation-indexed; same brackets for all filing statuses. The valid 2026 schedule = the statutory schedule (confirmed by the Va. Dept. of Taxation Tax Rate Schedule, Rev. 05/26). 2026 standard deduction is also statutory: $8,750 single/HoH, $17,500 MFJ (Va. Code § 58.1-322.03, fixed for 2025-2026; scheduled to sunset to $3k/$6k after 2026 unless extended — noted in disclaimer). Added as bracket type. No local/city wage tax in VA. Personal exemptions ($930) and the $12,000 age-65+ deduction not modeled (disclaimed). Scenario-tested via engine: single $75k -> $3,551.88 (matches hand-calc 60+60+600+0.0575*49250), monotonic/finite/non-negative across $40k/$75k/$150k/$600k x single/married/HoH. npm test green (36 passing).
Virginia.source: Official Va. Dept. of Taxation Tax Rate Schedule (Rev. 05/26) + Va. Code § 58.1-320 / § 58.1-322.03; validated vs SmartAsset/Talent.com consensus. https://www.tax.virginia.gov/ ; https://law.lis.virginia.gov/vacode/title58.1/chapter3/section58.1-320/

New Jersey.reason: NJ gross income tax is statutory (N.J.S.A. 54A:2-1), 1.4%-10.75%, fixed in law and NOT inflation-indexed; NJ Division of Taxation confirms no rate/bracket changes for 2025/2026, so the statutory schedule IS the valid 2026 schedule (unlike CA, which is inflation-indexed and unpublished). Schedule I (single/MFS) and Schedule II (MFJ/HoH, with extra 2.45% $50k-$70k band) added as bracket type. NJ has no standard deduction; personal/dependent exemptions not modeled (disclaimed). Scenario-tested via engine: single $75k -> $2,651.25 (matches hand-calc), monotonic/finite/non-negative across $40k/$75k/$150k/$600k x single/married/HoH. npm test green (36 passing).
New Jersey.source: Official NJ Division of Taxation rate schedules + new2025 changes page (no rate change); validated vs SmartAsset, incometaxpro, PaycheckCity/ustax.tools consensus. https://www.nj.gov/treasury/taxation/taxtables.shtml ; https://www.nj.gov/treasury/taxation/new2025.shtml

Ohio.reason: Added as bracket type from the OFFICIAL Ohio DOR 2026 Estimated Income Tax (ITES) worksheet: 0% to $26,050; 2.75% to $100,000; 3.125% over $100,000 (base amounts $0/$342/$2,394.32). HB 96 (signed 2025-06-30) phases Ohio toward a flat 2.75%, but TAX YEAR 2026 is the second phase-in year and the official 2026 worksheet RETAINS a 3.125% top band over $100,000 — the single flat 2.75% lands in a later year. RESOLVED a real conflict: many secondary sources (Plante Moran, ustax.tools, smartasset-adjacent) mislabel 2026 as already flat 2.75%; the official DOR worksheet (ranked first by procedure) overrides them. Same schedule for all filing statuses; Ohio's $1,900-per-exemption deduction and local municipal income taxes are state-only-disclaimed/not modeled. Modeled as marginal brackets. Scenario-tested via engine across $40k/$75k/$150k/$600k x single/married/HoH: all finite/non-negative/monotonic. Hand-verified single $75k -> $1,346.13 (=2.75% x ($75k-$26,050)) and single $150k -> $3,596.13 (=2.75% x $73,950 + 3.125% x $50,000), both exact. npm test green (36 passing).
Ohio.source: Official Ohio Dept. of Taxation 2026 Estimated Income Tax Payment Worksheet (ITES instructions), tax.ohio.gov; HB 96 FY2026-27 budget. Validated against official DOR worksheet over conflicting secondary sources. https://dam.assets.ohio.gov/image/upload/v1735926006/tax.ohio.gov/forms/ohio_individual/individual/2026/ites-instructions-fi.pdf
Ohio.caveat: Re-verify the 3.125% top band against the official 2026 IT 1040 instructions when DOR posts them; confirm whether the flat-2.75% step moves to 2027 or later.

Wisconsin.reason: Added as bracket type from the OFFICIAL Wisconsin Dept. of Revenue 2026 Tax Rate Schedules in the 2026 Form 1-ES instructions (D-101A, R. 1-26). WI is a graduated 4-bracket system (3.5%/4.4%/5.3%/7.65%), inflation-indexed AND officially PUBLISHED for tax year 2026 (so no 2026-pending flag needed). Schedule A (Single/HoH/Estates&Trusts): 3.5% to $15,110, 4.4% to $51,950, 5.3% to $332,720, 7.65% above. Schedule B (MFJ): 3.5% to $20,150, 4.4% to $69,260, 5.3% to $443,630, 7.65% above. RESOLVED a conflict: tax-rates.org and a 2023 LeMahieu proposal (AB1) claim WI moves to a FLAT 3.25% in 2026 — that bill was NOT enacted (partially vetoed); the official DOR 2026 schedule confirms WI remains graduated. ustax.tools competitor confirms the same 4-rate graduated structure (its thresholds lag at an earlier indexing year; official DOR used over it). CRITICAL SIMPLIFICATION: WI has NO fixed standard deduction — it uses an income-based sliding-scale deduction that phases out as income rises, which the engine cannot model; per the loop rules I used the supported schema with NO standard deduction and clearly disclaimed that estimates run slightly HIGH rather than fabricate a fixed figure. single key uses Schedule A (MFS Schedule C narrower bands disclaimed). No WI local income tax. Personal exemptions/credits not modeled. Scenario-tested via engine across $40k/$75k/$150k/$600k x single/married/HoH: all finite, non-negative, monotonic. Hand-verified single $75k -> $3,371.46 (= 3.5%x15110 + 4.4%x(51950-15110) + 5.3%x(75000-51950), no std deduction), exact. npm test green.
Wisconsin.source: Official Wisconsin Dept. of Revenue 2026 Form 1-ES instructions (D-101A, R. 1-26), 2026 Tax Rate Schedules + 2026 Standard Deduction schedules; validated vs ustax.tools (same graduated 4-rate structure). https://www.revenue.wi.gov/TaxForms2026/2026-Form1-ES-Inst.pdf ; https://www.revenue.wi.gov/Pages/FAQS/pcs-taxrates.aspx

Missouri.reason: RESOLVED the prior flag. Added as bracket type from the OFFICIAL Missouri Dept. of Revenue 2026 Withholding Tax Formula (effective Jan 1, 2026). The genuine TY2026 inflation-indexed percentage-method schedule is now confirmed: 0% to $1,348; 2.0% to $2,696; 2.5% to $4,044; 3.0% to $5,392; 3.5% to $6,740; 4.0% to $8,088; 4.5% to $9,436; 4.70% over $9,436 (taxable income after standard deduction). Same bracket schedule for all filing statuses; only the standard deduction differs. Standard deduction conforms to the FEDERAL TY2026 amounts: $16,100 single/MFS, $32,200 married, $24,150 HoH (these match this repo's federal block, Rev. Proc. 2025-32). The 4.70% top rate is unchanged for 2026 (SB3 phase-down holds; next cut to 4.6% is a later year). KEY: the prior competitor disagreement was the same 2025-mislabeled-as-2026 trap caught for CA/MN/OH — visaverge/AARP/etc. showed the TY2025 schedule (top band $9,191, std ded $15,750/$31,500/$23,625 = federal TY2025) under a 2026 label; the genuine TY2026 schedule (top band $9,436, std ded $16,100/$32,200/$24,150) is confirmed by multiple sources. The official dor.mo.gov Withholding Formula PDF remains Incapsula/WAF-blocked (HTTP 403), so the identical OFFICIAL 2026 schedule was obtained from the U.S. government USDA/NFC payroll bulletin (publishes Missouri's formula verbatim, effective PP08 2026 = Jan 1 2026) and validated vs halfpricesoft (identical 8-band bracket schedule) and Bloomberg Tax (standard deductions + 0-4.7% range). NOTE: Missouri's former deduction for a portion of federal income tax paid is ELIMINATED for 2026 (so the earlier 'federal-tax-deduction not modeled' caveat is moot). SIMPLIFICATIONS (state-only, disclaimed): the 1% LOCAL EARNINGS TAX in Kansas City and St. Louis is not modeled; age-65+/blind additional standard deduction, personal/dependent exemptions, and MO credits are not modeled. figureYear=2026 (genuine 2026 data, no prior-year fallback banner). Scenario-tested via engine across $40k/$75k/$150k/$600k x single/married/HoH: all finite, non-negative, monotonic. Hand-verified single $75k -> $2,587.668 exact (taxable=75000-16100=58900; exact marginal sum across all 8 bands; the DOR's rounded whole-dollar base amounts e.g. $263 differ by ~$0.14, which is expected — the engine uses exact marginal math). npm test green (exit 0, all suites incl. tax-data freshness).
Missouri.officialSource: https://dor.mo.gov/forms/Withholding%20Formula_2026.pdf (HTTP 403 / Incapsula-blocked) ; https://help.nfc.usda.gov/bulletins/2026/1773783048.htm (USDA/NFC publishes MO's official 2026 withholding formula verbatim)
Missouri.source: Official Missouri Dept. of Revenue 2026 Withholding Tax Formula (dor.mo.gov, Incapsula-blocked to automated fetch); identical official schedule retrieved from USDA/NFC payroll bulletin and validated vs halfpricesoft (brackets) and Bloomberg Tax (standard deductions/top rate). https://help.nfc.usda.gov/bulletins/2026/1773783048.htm ; https://dor.mo.gov/forms/Withholding%20Formula_2026.pdf

Connecticut.reason: Added as bracket type from the OFFICIAL Connecticut DRS Informational Publication 2026(7) "Is My Connecticut Withholding Correct?" (effective Jan 1-Dec 31 2026, issued 12/12/2025), Table B - Initial Tax Calculation. CT has 7 statutory rates (2/4.5/5.5/6/6.5/6.9/6.99%) under Conn. Gen. Stat. ch. 229; they are NOT inflation-indexed and were UNCHANGED for 2026 (the 2024 cut of the two lowest rates to 2%/4.5% is fully phased in), so the statutory schedule IS the valid 2026 schedule. Mapped CT-W4 withholding codes to our filing keys: single=Code A (2% to 10k ... 6.99% over 500k), married=Code C (2% to 20k ... 6.99% over 1M), head_of_household=Code B (2% to 16k ... 6.99% over 800k). RESOLVED a year-mislabel/stale trap: tax-rates.org still lists the pre-2024 3%/5% lowest rates (labeled 2023/2026) — rejected in favor of the official DRS 2026 publication. Validated single+MFJ vs Tax Foundation 2026 and ustax.tools (identical 7-rate 2%-6.99% schedule). SIMPLIFICATIONS per loop rules: CT has NO standard deduction (uses income-based personal exemptions 15k/24k/19k that phase out, plus personal tax credits) which the engine cannot model, so I used the supported schema with NO standard deduction and DISCLAIMED that estimates run slightly HIGH at lower incomes rather than fabricate a figure. CT 3% tax-rate phase-out (Table C add-back) and high-income tax recapture (Table D) are DISCLAIMED/not modeled (they can push effective rates above 6.99% for high earners). No CT local income tax. single key also covers MFS (CT Code F = Code A schedule). Scenario-tested via engine across 40k/75k/150k/600k x single/married/HoH: all finite, non-negative, monotonic. Hand-verified single 75k -> 3375.00 exact (= 2%x10000 + 4.5%x(50000-10000) + 5.5%x(75000-50000), no std deduction). npm test green (exit 0, all suites passing incl. tax-data freshness).
Connecticut.source: Official Connecticut DRS Informational Publication 2026(7), Table B - Initial Tax Calculation (effective Jan 1-Dec 31 2026); validated vs Tax Foundation 2026 + ustax.tools. https://portal.ct.gov/-/media/drs/publications/pubsip/2026/ip-2026-7.pdf ; https://taxfoundation.org/data/all/state/state-income-tax-rates-2026/

Oregon.reason: Added as bracket type from the OFFICIAL Oregon Dept. of Revenue 2026 Publication OR-ESTIMATE (150-101-026) tax rate charts AND the 2026 Oregon Withholding Tax Formulas (150-206-436, Rev. 12-31-25) — both extracted from the source PDFs (WebFetch's small model couldn't read the tables, so the PDFs were parsed locally with pdftotext). Oregon's 2026 schedule IS officially published. Four statutory rates 4.75%/6.75%/8.75%/9.9%. Chart S (single/MFS): 4.75% to $4,550, 6.75% to $11,400, 8.75% to $125,000, 9.9% above. Chart J (MFJ/HoH/QSS): 4.75% to $9,100, 6.75% to $22,800, 8.75% to $250,000, 9.9% above. Standard deduction 2026: $2,900 single/MFS, $5,800 MFJ/QSS, $4,650 HoH. HoH uses Chart J brackets but its own $4,650 std deduction (per the DOR charts) — modeled exactly that way. RESOLVED a year-mislabel/stale trap: ustax.tools (and some SmartAsset-adjacent sources) still show prior-year breakpoints ($4,050/$10,200 single, $8,100/$20,400 joint) under a 2026 label; the official DOR 2026 charts ($4,550/$11,400; $9,100/$22,800) override them. SIMPLIFICATIONS per loop rules, all disclaimed: Oregon's federal-tax subtraction (up to $8,750 for 2026, income-phased) is NOT modeled, so estimates run slightly HIGH for filers who claim it; the personal exemption credit ($260/exemption) and EIC are not modeled; Portland Metro/Multnomah County SEPARATE local personal income taxes are not modeled. Modeled STATE ONLY. Scenario-tested via engine across $40k/$75k/$150k/$600k x single/married/HoH: all finite, non-negative, monotonic. Hand-verified single $75k -> $5,989.75 exact (= 4.75%x4,550 + 6.75%x(11,400-4,550) + 8.75%x(72,100-11,400), taxable = 75,000-2,900; the DOR chart's rounded '$678' base = $678.50 exact). npm test green (exit 0, all suites incl. tax-data freshness passing).
Oregon.source: Official Oregon Dept. of Revenue 2026 Publication OR-ESTIMATE (150-101-026) rate charts + 2026 Oregon Withholding Tax Formulas (150-206-436, Rev. 12-31-25); cross-checked vs ustax.tools/Tax Foundation (which lagged at stale prior-year breakpoints — official DOR used over them). https://www.oregon.gov/dor/forms/FormsPubs/publication-or-estimate_101-026_2026.pdf ; https://www.oregon.gov/dor/forms/FormsPubs/withholding-tax-formulas_206-436_2026.pdf

Kansas.reason: Added as bracket type. Kansas TY2026 = two-bracket 5.20% / 5.58% schedule from Senate Bill 1 (June 2024 special session), which consolidated the prior three brackets (3.10%/5.25%/5.70%) into two. 5.20% on taxable income up to $23,000 single/HoH ($46,000 married filing jointly), 5.58% above. Tax Foundation's 2026 dataset lists exactly these rates/thresholds for Kansas, so figureYear=2026 (genuine 2026 data, no fallback banner). Standard deduction (SB1 fixed amounts, unchanged for 2026): $3,605 single/MFS, $8,240 MFJ, $6,180 HoH. RESOLVED a genuine conflict: LegalClarity claims Kansas uses 3.10%/5.70% with $30k/$60k thresholds for 2026 — those are the PRE-REFORM legacy rates (old 3.1% bottom / 5.7% top) mislabeled as current; rejected in favor of the Tax Foundation 2026 + AARP + EY tax-news + WebSearch consensus on 5.20%/5.58%. SB269's conditional phase-down toward 4.0% is trigger-gated to the state rainy-day fund hitting 15% and did NOT reduce the 2026 rates (TF still lists 5.20%/5.58% for 2026). HoH uses the single $23,000 threshold with its own $6,180 std deduction. CRITICAL SIMPLIFICATION (disclaimed): Kansas grants a LARGE separate PERSONAL EXEMPTION ($9,160 single/HoH, $18,320 MFJ, +$2,320/dependent) on top of the standard deduction, which the engine cannot model — so Kansas estimates run HIGH for most filers; disclaimed rather than fabricate. No KS local income tax; credits/age-65+ add-on not modeled. Scenario-tested via engine across $40k/$75k/$150k/$600k x single/married/HoH: all finite, non-negative, monotonic. Hand-verified single $75k -> $3,896.441 exact (taxable = 75000-3605 = 71395; = 5.2%x23000 + 5.58%x(71395-23000)). npm test green (exit 0).
Kansas.source: Tax Foundation 2026 state income tax rates (Kansas 5.20%/5.58%, thresholds + std deduction) + EY tax-news on Kansas SB1/SB269; validated vs AARP + WebSearch consensus. Official Kansas DOR KW-100 withholding booklet (Rev. 7-24) still shows pre-reform formula rates and timed out / lagged, so authoritative secondary + Tax Foundation used. https://taxfoundation.org/data/all/state/state-income-tax-rates-2026/ ; https://taxnews.ey.com/news/2025-1266-kansas-law-conditionally-lowers-personal-income-tax-rates-starting-in-2025

Nebraska.reason: Added under the APPROVED prior-year fallback policy with figureYear=2025. Nebraska's 2026 income-tax RATE schedule is NOT yet officially published: the state is mid-LB754 (2023) phase-down where only the TOP rate steps down (2024 5.84% -> 2025 5.20% -> 2026 4.55% -> 2027 3.99%) while the lower three rates (2.46%/3.51%/5.01%) are fixed and the bracket thresholds are inflation-indexed annually; the 2026 indexed thresholds have NOT been released (no 2026 Form 1040N rate schedule posted; the 2026 Circular EN gives only WITHHOLDING-table figures — rates 2.26/3.22/4.21/4.35/4.48/4.60% with allowance subtraction, which differ from the return rate schedule and are not the statutory brackets). Rather than mix the known 2026 top rate with unpublished 2026 thresholds (unverified), added the COMPLETE OFFICIAL 2025 schedule with figureYear=2025 + the unmistakable year-fallback banner (verified rendered on dist/nebraska-paycheck-calculator/index.html: '2025 rates (2026 pending). Showing Nebraska's official 2025 tax figures ...'), exactly as California was handled. Data is the verbatim OFFICIAL 2025 Nebraska Tax Calculation Schedule (Form 8-460-2025), parsed locally with pdftotext after WebFetch's reader failed on the PDF: Single/MFS 2.46% to $4,030, 3.51% to $24,120, 5.01% to $38,870, 5.20% above; MFJ/QSS 2.46% to $8,040, 3.51% to $48,250, 5.01% to $77,730, 5.20% above; HoH 2.46% to $7,510, 3.51% to $38,590, 5.01% to $57,630, 5.20% above. 2025 standard deduction (base, 0 boxes) from the official 2025 booklet chart: $8,600 single/MFS, $17,200 MFJ/QSS, $12,600 HoH. RESOLVED traps: tax-rates.org showed a stale 6.64% top rate (pre-2023); countrytaxcalc claimed a 'flat 5.84%' (Nebraska is graduated, and 5.84% was the 2024 top rate); visaverge's 2.46/3.51/4.55 thresholds ($2,400/$18,000) are withholding-table approximations, not the return schedule. SIMPLIFICATIONS (disclaimed, state-only): Nebraska's $171/exemption personal-exemption CREDIT, age-65+/blind additional std deduction, and the Social Security benefit exemption are not modeled. No NE local income tax. Scenario-tested via engine across $40k/$75k/$150k/$600k x single/married/HoH: all finite, non-negative, monotonic. Hand-verified single $75k -> $2,974.83 (taxable = 75000-8600 = 66400; = $1,543.276 base + 5.20% x (66400-38870); the schedule's rounded $1,543.28 base differs by ~$0.004, expected). npm test green (exit 0, all suites incl. freshness).
Nebraska.source: Official Nebraska Dept. of Revenue 2025 Tax Calculation Schedule (Form 8-460-2025, parsed locally via pdftotext) + 2025 Individual Income Tax Booklet standard-deduction chart; LB754 top-rate phase-down cross-checked vs Platte Institute / Governor's office / EY. 2026 return rate schedule not yet published (fallback to official 2025). https://revenue.nebraska.gov/sites/default/files/doc/tax-forms/2025/2025_Tax_Calculation_Schedule.pdf ; https://revenue.nebraska.gov/sites/default/files/doc/tax-forms/2025/f_Individual_Income_Tax_Booklet.pdf

South Carolina.reason: Added as bracket type from OFFICIAL South Carolina Dept. of Revenue guidance on H.4216 (Act 110, signed March 30, 2026), EFFECTIVE tax year 2026 (returns due April 15, 2027) — so genuine 2026 data, figureYear=2026, no fallback. H.4216 REPLACES SC's old 0%/3%/6% three-bracket system entirely for TY2026 with a two-rate schedule on federal-AGI-based taxable income: SCDOR states 'income less than $30,000 is 1.99%' and 'income from $30,000 and above is 5.21%, minus $966'. The 'minus $966' form is mathematically a clean marginal two-bracket schedule (verified continuity at $30,000: 1.99% x 30000 = $597 = 5.21% x 30000 - 966), modeled as 1.99% to $30,000, 5.21% above, SAME rates for all filing statuses. SCDOR also created the new SC Income-Adjusted Deduction (SCIAD) replacing the federal standard/itemized deduction: $15,000 single/MFS, $22,500 HoH, $30,000 MFJ/surviving spouse — modeled as the standardDeduction. RESOLVED A GENUINE SOURCE CONFLICT (this was the kind that would otherwise force a flag): Tax Foundation 2026 still lists SC's OLD 0%/3%/6% system (top reverting to 6.2% on 7/1/2026, federal std deduction $8,350/$16,700) — it PREDATES H.4216, signed only 2.5 months ago. The official SCDOR H.4216 page (ranked first per loop procedure) authoritatively overrides the stale Tax Foundation row, and PolicyEngine + ITEP corroborate the 1.99%/5.21% + SCIAD structure, so this is official+corroborated, not a genuine unresolved disagreement. SIMPLIFICATIONS (disclaimed): SCIAD PHASES DOWN at higher incomes (engine can't model) — used full SCIAD, so actual tax can be slightly HIGHER for high earners; SC starts from federal AGI and the estimate approximates taxable income as wages minus SCIAD (federal-AGI adjustments not modeled); the capped state EITC ($200) and other SC credits not modeled; future revenue-triggered rate cuts not pre-applied. No SC local income tax. Scenario-tested via engine across $40k/$75k/$150k/$600k x single/married/HoH: all finite, non-negative, monotonic. Hand-verified single $75k -> $2,160.00 exact (taxable = 75000-15000 = 60000; = 1.99% x 30000 + 5.21% x 30000 = 597 + 1563; engine returned 2160.00). npm test green (exit 0, all suites incl. freshness).
South Carolina.source: Official South Carolina Dept. of Revenue H.4216 / Act 110 guidance page (effective TY2026); corroborated by PolicyEngine SC-2026 + ITEP. Tax Foundation 2026 row is pre-H.4216 (old 0/3/6% + 6.2% reversion) and was superseded by the official SCDOR ruling. https://dor.sc.gov/news/information-about-h-4216 ; https://www.policyengine.org/us/south-carolina-2026-tax-changes

Alabama.reason: Added as bracket type. Alabama's individual income tax is the statutory three-bracket 2%/4%/5% schedule (Ala. Code § 40-18-5), unchanged for 2026 — no rate change was enacted, so the long-standing statutory schedule IS the valid 2026 schedule (figureYear=2026, no fallback). Single/MFS/HoH: 2% to $500, 4% to $3,000, 5% above. MFJ (exactly 2x single): 2% to $1,000, 4% to $6,000, 5% above. Confirmed identical across Tax Foundation 2026, halfpricesoft 2026, icalculator 2026, AARP 2026. Standard deduction modeled at the MAX amounts (single $3,000, MFJ $8,500, HoH $5,200, MFS $4,250 — efile/halfpricesoft/icalculator/nationaltaxreports consensus). CRITICAL SIMPLIFICATION: Alabama's standard deduction is INCOME-PHASED (DOR Standard Deduction Chart, Form 40) — it declines as Alabama AGI rises toward a lower floor, which the engine cannot model; used the max and disclaimed that actual tax can be slightly HIGHER than shown at higher incomes (a smaller phased-down deduction => more taxable income). Personal/dependent exemptions ($1,500/$3,000 + $1,000/dependent), the federal-income-tax deduction Alabama allows, and the new 2026 overtime-pay subtraction (up to $1,000) not modeled. LOCAL occupational (wage) taxes in some cities (~1% Birmingham/Gadsden/Macon Co.) not modeled. Scenario-tested via engine across $40k/$75k/$150k/$600k x single/married/HoH: all finite, non-negative, monotonic. Hand-verified single $75k -> $3,560.00 exact (taxable = 75000-3000 = 72000; = 2%x500 + 4%x2500 + 5%x(72000-3000); engine returned 3560.00). npm test green (exit 0, all suites incl. freshness).
Alabama.source: Statutory Ala. Code § 40-18-5 (2%/4%/5%, unchanged 2026); rates/std-deduction validated vs Tax Foundation 2026 + halfpricesoft 2026 + icalculator 2026 + AARP 2026; std-deduction phase-down per Alabama DOR Standard Deduction Chart (Form 40). https://taxfoundation.org/data/all/state/state-income-tax-rates-2026/ ; https://www.revenue.alabama.gov/forms/standard-deduction-chart-40-3/

Arkansas.reason: Added as bracket type from the OFFICIAL Arkansas Dept. of Finance and Administration 2026 Withholding Tax Formula (whformula_2026.pdf, Effective 01/01/2026), parsed locally with pdftotext (WebFetch couldn't read the binary PDF). GENUINE 2026 data (figureYear=2026, no fallback): DFA raised the 2026 standard deduction to $2,470 (from 2025's $2,410) and the top rate stays 3.9%. The official 'percentage minus adjustment' table for net taxable income up to $94,700 is mathematically identical to a marginal-bracket schedule — verified each band's minus-adjustment reconciles to continuity at the breakpoint (e.g. at $11,200: 2%x11200-111.98 = 3%x11200-223.97; at $16,000: 3%x16000-223.97 = 3.4%x16000-287.97; at $26,400: 3.4%x26400-287.97 = 3.9%x26400-419.96). Modeled as marginal brackets: 0% to $5,599, 2% to $11,199, 3% to $15,999, 3.4% to $26,399, 3.9% above. SAME schedule for all filing statuses (Arkansas does not vary brackets by filing status); only the standard deduction differs ($2,470 per taxpayer single/MFS/HoH, $4,940 two-earner MFJ, Ark. Code 26-51-430). Validated vs Tax Foundation 2026 (compressed high-income 2-bracket form, top 3.9%, std ded $2,470/$4,940) and halfpricesoft 2026 (identical 0/2/3/3.4/3.9% table + std ded $2,470). SIMPLIFICATIONS (disclaimed, state-only): the $29-per-exemption personal tax CREDIT and dependent credits not modeled (estimates run slightly high for typical filers); Arkansas's high-income 'bracket adjustment' that tapers away the lower-bracket benefit above ~$94,700 (effective rate -> flat 3.9% on all income) is NOT modeled, so estimates above ~$95k run modestly LOW. No AR local income tax. Scenario-tested via engine across $40k/$75k/$150k/$600k x single/married/HoH: all finite, non-negative, monotonic. Hand-verified single $75k -> $2,408.71 exact (= official formula 3.9% x (75000-2470) - $419.96; engine returned 2408.709). npm test green (exit 0, all suites incl. freshness).
Arkansas.source: Official Arkansas DFA 2026 Withholding Tax Formula (whformula_2026.pdf, Eff. 01/01/2026), parsed locally via pdftotext; validated vs Tax Foundation 2026 + halfpricesoft 2026. https://www.dfa.arkansas.gov/wp-content/uploads/whformula_2026.pdf ; https://taxfoundation.org/data/all/state/state-income-tax-rates-2026/

Oklahoma.reason: Added under the APPROVED prior-year fallback policy with figureYear=2025. Oklahoma ENACTED a 2026 change (HB2764, signed May 28 2025) cutting the top rate 4.75% -> 4.50% and consolidating brackets effective Jan 1 2026, BUT the exact 2026 statutory return-schedule thresholds are not reliably published: the 2026 OW-2 withholding packet is binary/WAF-blocked and only gives withholding approximations (2.5/3.5/4.5%); the official HB2764 bill summary says brackets drop 'from six to FOUR' but lists NO thresholds; secondaries disagree (three vs four brackets; ustax.tools shows the stale 2025 six-bracket schedule mislabeled as 2026; incometaxbystate shows a coherent-but-unconfirmable three-bracket 2026 schedule 0.25/2.75/4.5 with std ded $7,350/$14,700). Since 2026 cannot be confirmed from an authoritative source, added the COMPLETE OFFICIAL 2025 schedule with figureYear=2025 + the unmistakable year-fallback banner (verified rendered on dist/oklahoma-paycheck-calculator/index.html). 2025 data is from the official OTC Form 511 packet (2025 tax tables + '$100,000 or more' worksheet), parsed locally via pdftotext; bracket thresholds were verified by reproducing the worksheet bases EXACTLY: single $100k tax = $4,562 (computed $4,561.50, rounds) and MFJ/HoH/QW $100k tax = $4,373 (computed $4,373.00 exact). Single/MFS: 0.25% to $1,000, 0.75% to $2,500, 1.75% to $3,750, 2.75% to $4,900, 3.75% to $7,200, 4.75% above. MFJ, HoH AND QSS SHARE ONE schedule (per the OTC worksheet grouping): 0.25% to $2,000, 0.75% to $5,000, 1.75% to $7,500, 2.75% to $9,800, 3.75% to $14,400, 4.75% above — note this is NOT simply 2x single (the top band starts at $14,400, confirmed by solving the $4,373 base). 2025 std deduction: $6,350 single/MFS, $12,700 MFJ/QSS, $9,350 HoH. SIMPLIFICATIONS (disclaimed, state-only): Oklahoma's $1,000/person personal exemption, child/child-care credit, EIC, and sales-tax-relief credit are not modeled. No OK local income tax. Scenario-tested via engine across $40k/$75k/$150k/$600k x single/married/HoH: all finite, non-negative, monotonic. Hand-verified single $75k -> $3,072.38 exact (taxable = 75000-6350 = 68650; $153.50 lower-band base + 4.75% x (68650-7200)). npm test green (exit 0).
Oklahoma.source: Official Oklahoma Tax Commission 2025 Form 511 packet (2025 income tax tables + $100,000-or-more tax computation worksheet + standard-deduction instructions), parsed locally via pdftotext. HB2764 (2026 rate cut) cross-checked vs the official oklegislature.gov bill summary + Tax Foundation. 2026 return thresholds not verifiably published (fallback to official 2025). https://oklahoma.gov/content/dam/ok/en/tax/documents/forms/individuals/current/511-Pkt.pdf

Maine.reason: Added as bracket type from the OFFICIAL Maine Revenue Services 2026 Individual Income Tax Rate Schedules (ind_tax_rate_sched_2026.pdf, MRS Income/Estate Tax Division, Revised May 5, 2026), parsed locally via pdftotext after WebFetch's reader failed on the binary PDF. GENUINE 2026 data (figureYear=2026, no fallback): Maine's brackets and standard deduction are inflation-indexed (36 M.R.S. § 5403) and the 2026 schedule IS officially published. Three statutory rates 5.8%/6.75%/7.15% PLUS a NEW 2% surcharge (effective tax years on/after Jan 1, 2026) on Maine taxable income over $1,000,000 single / $1,500,000 MFJ&HoH / $750,000 MFS — modeled as a fourth marginal band at 9.15% (=7.15%+2%) at those thresholds. Single/MFS: 5.8% to $27,400, 6.75% to $64,850, 7.15% to $1,000,000, 9.15% above. MFJ/surviving spouse: 5.8% to $54,850, 6.75% to $129,750, 7.15% to $1,500,000, 9.15% above. HoH: 5.8% to $41,100, 6.75% to $97,300, 7.15% to $1,500,000, 9.15% above. 2026 std deduction (official MRS): $15,700 single/MFS, $31,400 MFJ, $23,550 HoH. RESOLVED a source conflict: Tax Foundation 2026 lists std deduction $8,350/$16,700 — those are Maine's lower phase-out FLOOR amounts, not the base 2026 std deduction; the official MRS schedule ($15,700/$31,400/$23,550) ranks first and is used; TF otherwise CONFIRMS the 5.8/6.75/7.15% rates + identical thresholds ($27,400/$64,850 single; $54,850/$129,750 MFJ). SIMPLIFICATIONS (disclaimed, state-only): Maine's std deduction + $5,300 personal exemption phase out for high earners (engine can't model — estimates run slightly LOW for high earners); MFS $750,000 surcharge threshold not modeled under our shared 'single' key; age/blindness add-on + credits not modeled. No ME local income tax. Scenario-tested via engine across $40k/$75k/$150k/$600k/$1.6M x single/married/HoH: all finite, non-negative, monotonic. Hand-verified single $75k -> $3,742.45 exact (taxable = 75000-15700 = 59300; = 5.8% x 27400 + 6.75% x (59300-27400) = 1589.20 + 2153.25; engine returned 3742.45). npm test green (exit 0, all suites incl. freshness).
Maine.source: Official Maine Revenue Services 2026 Individual Income Tax Rate Schedules (ind_tax_rate_sched_2026.pdf, Rev. May 5 2026), parsed locally via pdftotext; validated vs Tax Foundation 2026 (rates + bracket thresholds match; TF std-deduction figure is Maine's phase-out floor, superseded by official MRS). https://www.maine.gov/revenue/sites/maine.gov.revenue/files/inline-files/ind_tax_rate_sched_2026.pdf ; https://taxfoundation.org/data/all/state/state-income-tax-rates-2026/

Hawaii.reason: Added as bracket type from the OFFICIAL Hawaii Dept. of Taxation Tax Rate Schedules 'For Taxable Years Beginning After December 31, 2024' (Schedule I/II/III, tax.hawaii.gov rate-schedule page) for the brackets, plus official DOTAX Announcement No. 2025-07 (Dec 1, 2025) for the 2026 standard deduction. GENUINE 2026 data (figureYear=2026, no fallback): Act 46 (SLH 2024) widened Hawaii's 12 brackets for tax year 2025 and RAISES the standard deduction again for 2026; Ann. 2025-07 confirms the bracket schedule is otherwise unchanged for 2026, so the post-2024 widened schedule IS the valid 2026 bracket schedule. Twelve rates 1.4/3.2/5.5/6.4/6.8/7.2/7.6/7.9/8.25/9/10/11%. Schedule I (single/MFS) thresholds 9600/14400/19200/24000/36000/48000/125000/175000/225000/275000/325000; Schedule II (MFJ/surviving spouse) = 2x single (19200/28800/38400/48000/72000/96000/250000/350000/450000/550000/650000); Schedule III (HoH) = 1.5x single (14400/21600/28800/36000/54000/72000/187500/262500/337500/412500/487500) — all extracted verbatim from the official rate-schedule page, base-dollar amounts reconcile to the marginal sum. 2026 standard deduction (Act 46, per Ann. 2025-07): $8,000 single/MFS, $16,000 MFJ/surviving spouse, $12,000 HoH (confirmed by Bank of Hawaii + nationaltaxreports). SIMPLIFICATIONS (disclaimed, state-only): personal exemptions ($1,144/exemption, doubled age 65+), itemized deductions, and credits (food/excise, EITC, child & dependent care) not modeled. No Hawaii local/county income tax. Scenario-tested via engine across $40k/$75k/$150k/$600k x single/married/HoH: all finite, non-negative, monotonic. Hand-verified single $75k -> $3,983.20 (taxable = 75000-8000 = 67000; exact marginal sum $2,539.20 + 7.6% x (67000-48000); DOTAX's rounded $2,539 base differs by $0.20, expected). npm test green (exit 0, all suites incl. freshness).
Hawaii.source: Official Hawaii Dept. of Taxation Tax Rate Schedules (post-Dec-31-2024, Schedule I/II/III) + DOTAX Announcement No. 2025-07 (Dec 1 2025, 2026 standard deduction $8,000/$16,000/$12,000); validated vs Bank of Hawaii + nationaltaxreports (std deduction) and the official Schedule I/II/III thresholds. https://tax.hawaii.gov/forms/d_25table-on/d_25table-on_p13/ ; https://files.hawaii.gov/tax/news/announce/ann25-07.pdf

Rhode Island.reason: Added as bracket type. RI is a three-bracket graduated state (3.75%/4.75%/5.99%) with the SAME bracket schedule for ALL filing statuses (only the standard deduction varies); brackets are inflation-indexed annually and the 2026 figures ARE officially published (figureYear=2026, no fallback). Official source = RI Division of Taxation Advisory 2025-22 (ADV 2025-22, issued 11/03/2025). The tax.ri.gov advisory PDF is Cloudflare/WAF-protected: HTTP 403 to WebFetch/curl, and in Chrome it showed a 'Verify you are human' CAPTCHA which I did NOT bypass (per safety rules). So figures were taken from authoritative sources publishing ADV 2025-22 verbatim: the advisory confirms the first bracket reaches $82,050 (3.75%) and the std-deduction/exemption phase-out begins at MAGI $254,250; Tax Foundation 2026 supplies the full schedule + std deduction: 3.75% to $82,050, 4.75% to $186,450, 5.99% above; std deduction $11,200 single, $22,400 MFJ. HoH std deduction ($16,800) is DERIVED from RI's documented structure (HoH = single + half the single-to-MFJ gap = 11,200 + (22,400-11,200)/2), which reproduces RI's published 2024 HoH ($15,850) exactly — documented RI pattern, not fabricated, disclaimed as derived. SIMPLIFICATIONS (disclaimed, state-only): RI std deduction + personal exemptions PHASE OUT entirely above MAGI $254,250 (engine can't model — estimates run LOW for high earners); ~$5,100/exemption personal exemptions + RI credits not modeled. No RI local income tax. Scenario-tested via engine across $40k/$75k/$150k/$250k/$600k x single/married/HoH: all finite, non-negative, monotonic. Hand-verified single $75k -> $2,392.50 exact (taxable = 75000-11200 = 63800; entirely in 3.75% band = 3.75% x 63800; engine returned 2392.50). npm test green (exit 0, all suites incl. freshness).
Rhode Island.source: RI Division of Taxation Advisory 2025-22 (ADV 2025-22, 11/03/2025, 2026 inflation-adjusted amounts) — official PDF Cloudflare/WAF-blocked (403 + CAPTCHA, not bypassed); figures from Bloomberg Tax (announces ADV 2025-22: first bracket $82,050, phase-out MAGI $254,250) + Tax Foundation 2026 (full 3.75/4.75/5.99% schedule, thresholds $82,050/$186,450, std deduction $11,200/$22,400). HoH std deduction derived from RI's documented mid-point structure. https://news.bloombergtax.com/daily-tax-report-state/rhode-island-tax-division-announces-2026-inflation-adjusted-personal-income-tax-amounts ; https://taxfoundation.org/data/all/state/state-income-tax-rates-2026/
Rhode Island.caveat: Re-verify the HoH standard deduction ($16,800, currently structurally derived) and confirm all four filing-status std deductions against ADV 2025-22 or the official 2026 RI-1040 instructions when a machine-readable copy is reachable (tax.ri.gov is Cloudflare-gated).
